name: Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque
name_original: Bosque Las Majadas y árboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque
region_intro: Hiking trails around San Pedro De Ceque, a municipality in Zamora province, Spain, traverse a diverse landscape of fields, hills, and river valleys. The region is characterized by its natural environment, including the notable Bosque de las Majadas, home to ancient oaks, holm oaks, and centenary ash trees. Trails often follow the valleys of the Tera and Vidriales rivers, showcasing fertile floodplains and traditional agricultural areas. This varied terrain provides opportunities for exploring rural surroundings and natural heritage. Best hiking trails around San Pedro De Ceque The most popular hiking route is Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route leads through the ancient Bosque de las Majadas, known for its old-growth trees. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ancient Trees of Las Majadas Trail, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.7 km) path. It offers a journey through the region's historic forests, highlighting ancient trees and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Petavonium Roman Camp Trail, a 6.7 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around San Pedro de Ceque is defined by ancient oak and holm oak forests, river valleys, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. 7 hikers have used komoot to explore San Pedro De Ceque's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in San Pedro De Ceque?
A: There are 8 hiking trails around San Pedro De Ceque, offering a range of experiences from easy walks to moderate excursions. This includes 1 easy route and 7 moderate routes, ensuring options for various fitness levels.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in San Pedro De Ceque?
A: The ideal seasons for hiking in San Pedro De Ceque are spring and autumn. During these times, the climate is temperate, providing comfortable conditions to explore the region's natural beauty, including its ancient forests and river valleys.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on hikes around San Pedro De Ceque?
A: Hikes in San Pedro De Ceque often feature a mosaic of fields, hills, and the valleys of the Tera and Vidriales rivers. A significant highlight is the &lt;strong&gt;Bosque de las Majadas&lt;/strong&gt;, home to ancient, thick-trunked oaks, holm oaks, and centenary ash trees. You'll also encounter fertile floodplains and traditional agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in San Pedro De Ceque?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy hiking route suitable for families. The trails generally traverse gentle terrain, making them accessible for a wider range of ages and abilities. For a specific easy option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24310588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera loop from Camarzana de Tera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several circular routes allow you to start and end in the same location. Notable options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022496865/bosque-las-majadas-y-arboles-milenarios-vuelta-desde-san-pedro-de-ceque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305488286/camino-sanabres-olleros-de-tera-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés: Olleros de Tera Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural points of interest can be found near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural landscapes, San Pedro De Ceque offers cultural and historical sites. The village itself has a well-maintained Plaza Mayor, the Ermita de la Vera Cruz, and the Ermita de la Virgen de las Nieves. The town's central church features Gothic and Renaissance elements. You can also explore nearby attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The diverse terrain around San Pedro De Ceque supports a variety of wildlife. Hikers have the chance to spot deer, wild boars, and various bird species, especially in the more secluded forest areas like the Bosque de las Majadas and along the river valleys.

Q: What is the terrain like on the hiking trails?
A: The terrain around San Pedro De Ceque is varied, encompassing a mix of fields, rolling hills, and river valleys. Trails often follow the fertile floodplains of the Tera and Vidriales rivers, and lead through ancient oak and holm oak forests. Most routes are moderate, with gentle elevation changes.

Q: Are there any specific trails that highlight the ancient trees of the region?
A: Yes, the region is renowned for its ancient forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022496865/bosque-las-majadas-y-arboles-milenarios-vuelta-desde-san-pedro-de-ceque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bosque Las Majadas y arboles milenarios — vuelta desde San Pedro de Ceque&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305973560/ancient-trees-of-las-majadas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Trees of Las Majadas Trail&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to showcase the impressive old-growth oaks, holm oaks, and centenary ash trees of the Bosque de las Majadas.

Q: What other attractions are there to explore near San Pedro De Ceque?
A: Beyond the hiking trails, you can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3941370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Pilgrim of Rionegro del Puente&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the tranquility of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, or find a relaxing spot at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3858806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in San Pedro De Ceque?
A: Hikers frequently appreciate the tranquil rural surroundings and the rich natural heritage, particularly the ancient forests like the Bosque de las Majadas. The varied landscapes, from river valleys to agricultural fields, also provide a diverse and engaging experience for those exploring the trails.
